page_title: Wage overtime and meal break law — Michigan
meta_title: Michigan Wage, Overtime and Meal-Break Law 2026 | Teamed
meta_description: Michigan wage law 2026: $13.73/hr minimum (Improved Workforce Opportunity Wage Act), FLSA-only overtime, no adult meal-break statute, final pay next regular payday under MCL 408.475. Tipped wage $5.49.
target_keywords: michigan wage overtime and meal break law 2026
audit_score_notes: Score: 90/100. Deductions: prompt_correction flag (-5), factual_uncertainty:medium on post-2026 rate schedule (-3), image_fallback placeholder in use (-2).

prompt_corrections.original_prompt_claim: Improved Workforce Opportunity Wage Act ballot schedule from Proposal 22-2 (2023) sets trajectory toward $15.00 with tipped credit eliminated by 2028
prompt_corrections.verified_fact: Michigan legislature amended the schedule via HB 4001 and HB 4002 (February 2024). The tipped credit was PRESERVED (not eliminated). The 2026 rate of $13.73 is confirmed by MI LEO December 2025 press release. Future-year rates beyond 2026 are not yet legislatively confirmed.
prompt_corrections.source_authority: Michigan Department of Labor and Economic Opportunity

factual_uncertainty_flags.description: The minimum-wage rate trajectory beyond 2026 (i.e., 2027+ figures under the post-HB 4001/HB 4002 schedule) has not been legislatively confirmed as of May 2026. The compliance-cache michigan.json records only the 2026 figure of $13.73. Page does not assert a 2027 or 2028 rate. Re-verify at each annual LEO announcement.
factual_uncertainty_flags.action: Re-verify against MI LEO press release each December for following year rate.

factual_uncertainty_flags.description: The 2026-2028 overtime and tip income exemption from Michigan 4.25% state income tax is referenced in the compliance-cache michigan.json and the MI Treasury 2026 Withholding Guide. Page applies both but notes the exemption expires 31 December 2028. Confirm against Michigan Treasury guidance if payroll runs after December 2028.

MCL 408.475 cited as the final-pay-timing statute. This is consistent with the compliance-cache entry citing MCL 408.475. Cross-reference against the full PWFBA (MCL 408.471-408.490) if using for a specific client matter.
factual_uncertainty_flags.action: Verify MCL 408.475 still governs final-pay timing specifically (not moved to a different section) before citing in client documentation.

scheduled_data_updates.trigger: Each December (Michigan LEO minimum-wage press release)
scheduled_data_updates.note: Michigan LEO typically announces the following-year minimum wage in November-December. Update the capsule, tables, and meta description before 1 January.
The 2026-2028 overtime and tip income exemptions expire on 31 December 2028. Remove exemption references from the page on that date unless extended.

editor_rubric_checks.crossover_signal_text: EOR is the right structure for Michigan until it isn't
tom_tagline_facts: Michigan raised its minimum wage to $13.73 from 1 January 2026
2024 HB 4001/HB 4002 legislative rewrite preserved the tipped credit
Michigan temporarily exempted overtime pay from the 4.25% state income tax through 2028
